title: The Present Perfect
description: Link the past to now with have/has + past participle: experiences (ever/never), recent actions (just/already/yet) and unfinished time (for/since) — and when to use it instead of the past simple.
sources: CEFR B1 descriptors (Council of Europe, Common European Framework of Reference)
Raymond Murphy, English Grammar in Use (present perfect; present perfect vs past simple)
